subject: Computer Networking
title: CC, BCC, and Reply-All
description: One-minute skill: CC, BCC, and Reply-All.

hook.text: CC, BCC, Reply-All — simple buttons that cause massive confusion and even data leaks.

buildup.text: CC sends a copy to additional recipients. Everyone can see who's on CC. It's a visible courtesy copy.

discovery.text: BCC hides recipients from each other. Servers strip BCC addresses before delivery. Invisible copies.

twist.text: Reply-All responds to every address. One careless click can send personal opinions to the whole company.

climax.text: In 2003, a Reply-All storm at a large firm generated 15 million emails in hours. Servers crashed.

punchline.text: BCC hides, CC shows, Reply-All broadcasts. Choose carefully.

quiz.question: What does BCC do that CC does not?
options: Hides the recipient's address from all other recipients
Sends the email at a higher priority
